pattern: DFS to mark one island, then BFS expanding outward from every marked cell to find the shortest path to the second island.
hints: Locate any cell of the first island with a simple scan, then use DFS/BFS to collect all its cells and add them to a BFS queue.
Run a multi-source BFS from all cells of the first island simultaneously, expanding into water. Track the number of water cells crossed (the bridge length).
The first time BFS reaches a cell belonging to the second island, the current distance is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: two islands separated by a row of water
islands at opposite corners, need many flips
first island is a ring, second is inside — distance 1
second island is large multi-cell block

stress.description: 100x100 grid with two constructed rectangular islands
